sanskrit:
कार्यमित्येव यत्कर्म नियतं क्रियतेऽर्जुन।
सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलं चैव स त्यागः सात्त्विको मतः॥
transliteration:
kāryam ity eva yat karma niyataṃ kriyate ’rjuna |
saṅgaṃ tyaktvā phalaṃ caiva sa tyāgaḥ sāttviko mataḥ ||
literal_meaning: That action which is performed simply because it ‘must be done,’ prescribed, giving up attachment and also the fruit—this renunciation is deemed sāttvic.
contextual_meaning: Healthy functioning is doing the right thing because it aligns with one’s values, not for reward or relief from guilt; outcome-detachment prevents burnout and moral fatigue.
psychological_state: Flow-like engagement driven by internalized values; ego kept out of the result.
trigger_scenario: Staying late to finish a report because accuracy matters, not because the boss is watching or for overtime pay.
anchor_text: Do what must be done, let go of the outcome.
